commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a gateway startup predicate that loads non-bundled manifest channel plugins when their effective activation state is explicitly enabled.

PR surface: Source +64. Total +64 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main scopes Gateway runtime loading to startup.pluginIds, and a manifest channel plugin enabled only through plugins.entries.<id>.enabled=true does not currently enter that plan. I did not run the macOS/Homebrew WeChat live setup.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed because automation cannot provide the external contributor’s live installed-plugin proof, and maintainers should choose whether to consolidate with the related open startup PRs.

target.title: fix(plugins): load explicitly enabled externally-installed channel plugins at gateway startup
target.body:
## Summary

- Externally-installed npm channel plugins (e.g., WeChat, Telegram) enabled via `plugins.entries.<id>.enabled=true` were never loaded at gateway startup if their channels were not yet configured in the `channels` section and they didn't match any other startup predicate.
- Root cause: `resolveGatewayStartupPluginPlanFromRegistry` only checked manifest-based predicates (channel/provider/hook/harness/`activation.onStartup`) and had no fallback for explicitly enabled external channel plugins without channel config.
- Adds `canStartExplicitlyEnabledPlugin` predicate narrowed to **externally-installed channel plugins only** (checks `listManifestChannelIds`), preserving lazy behavior for non-channel plugins like disabled web search, `activation.onStartup=false`, and unselected context-engine plugins.
- Fixes #93219

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts` — 144/144 tests pass
- `pnpm test src/plugins/bundled-plugin-metadata.test.ts src/plugins/config-state.test.ts src/plugins/config-contracts.test.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index.test.ts src/plugins/discovery.test.ts` — 342/342 tests pass
- CI: pending (real behavior proof check expected)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Externally-installed npm channel plugins silently skipped during gateway startup when enabled via plugins.entries.<id>.enabled=true but channel not configured
Real environment tested: Source checkout, pnpm test suite with existing channel-plugin-ids fixture
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm test src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts
Evidence after fix: 144/144 tests pass; canStartExplicitlyEnabledPlugin with manifestLookup check catches non-bundled channel plugins with enabled=true while rejecting non-channel plugins via listManifestChannelIds. 3 reverted tests confirm disabled web search, activation.onStartup=false, and unselected context-engine plugins stay lazy.
Observed result after fix: External channel plugins with enabled=true are added to gateway startup plan. Non-channel external plugins remain gated by their existing activation predicates.
What was not tested: Full integration test with a real externally-installed npm plugin in a live gateway (requires macOS + WeChat setup)
target.author: xialonglee
